page: Microsoft Azure A4_v2 is an entry-level virtual machine belonging to the Av2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The server is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, providing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 41 GB, and there are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data places the A4_v2 in the bottom 10% across all evaluated performance categories. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng yielded scores of 348.12 and 1343.44 respectively, while Geekbench recorded a single-core score of 334.0 and a multi-core score of 1079.0. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block transfers measuring at 12.94 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 9.56 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks show similar constraints, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 467.07 and single-threaded gzip compression at 8.51 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the A4_v2 represents a low-tier hardware profile suitable for budget-conscious scenarios where high-throughput performance is not required. The resource density is modest, making it a poor fit for demanding database operations, heavy compilation, or real-time data processing. Instead, this virtual machine is best utilized for light development environments, low-traffic web serving, basic utility scripting, or small-scale testing workloads that do not demand high memory bandwidth or intensive CPU calculations.
description: Microsoft Azure A4_v2 is an entry-level virtual machine from the Av2 family, utilizing an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 41 GB of local storage. The server does not include a GPU. Across standardized benchmarks, including stress-ng and Geekbench, the A4_v2 consistently performs in the bottom 10% for both compute and memory bandwidth, with large-block memory reads reaching 12.94 GB/sec. Given these performance constraints, this server is positioned as a budget-friendly option for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, light development tasks, and non-critical testing environments rather than resource-heavy database or computational applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A4_v2 is an entry-level virtual machine featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 41 GB of local storage, designed for low-intensity workloads and basic development tasks.

tagline: An entry-level virtual machine with dedicated Intel cores and local storage, configured for low-intensity development and basic utility workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory and 41 GB of bundled local storage
Low-tier performance profile falling in the bottom 10% of benchmarks
